name: John Mayall
abstract: John Brumwell Mayall (29 November 1933 – 22 July 2024) was an English blues and rock musician, songwriter and producer. In the 1960s, he formed John Mayall & the Bluesbreakers, a band that has counted among its members some of the most famous blues and blues rock musicians of all time.

concerts.label: John Mayall, Delbert & Glen, and Tranquility
Kool Jazz Festival: The Johnny Otis Show

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him. We would rehearse the stuff at his house... even when I worked with John Mayall... I enjoyed working with him. I made more money with John Mayall than any big band I ever worked with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript mentions John Mayall only in a list of artists who covered 'Parchman Farm' provided by the interviewer; Mose Allison does not mention any personal interaction or relationship with John Mayall.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: everybody was into Led Zeppelin and John Mayall and the Heartbreakers and the Beatles and the Stones and stuff
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Laurence Juber
about_quotes.evidence: I was also doing the same thing with Eric Clapton with John Mayall’s Blues Breakers records
about_quotes.doc_title: Laurence Juber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Glasgow, Scotland, July 27, 2016 [interview 1 of 2]
